title: Unidentified clone trooper (Brentaal IV)
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image=[[File:ShaakTisTrooper.jpg]] |name=Unidentified clone trooper
(Brentaal IV) |homeworld=[[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]{{DB|organization|clonetroopers|clone troopers}} |birth= |death=[[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Clone Wars)|Battle of Brentaal IV]] to [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]]. [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]]''[[Jedi: Shaak Ti]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Cloning/Legends|clone]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |donor=[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] |height=1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Tan |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|They left me…for dead. And I will be…soon. Weapon locked on overload. I have done…my duty…to the best of…my ability.|The trooper, just before his death|}} A '''clone trooper''' was one of the many [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] who served in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. In [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], he participated in the [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Clone Wars)|Battle of Brentaal IV]] under the command of [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]]. Spurred by a fake intelligence report, the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] rushed in to retake [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]] from clan leader [[Shogar Tok]], who was affiliated with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. However, the Republic [[Troop/Legends|troops]] found themselves under heavy fire from Tok's [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] and Confederate [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. Separated from the rest of their forces, Ti, the [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]], and two more clone soldiers retreated into a nearby [[Brentaal IV prison|prison]] facility, where they assisted three [[Prisoner/Legends|inmates]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Sagoro Autem]], the [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]] [[Lyshaa]], and the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Ryyk (Wookiee)|Ryyk]], in defeating the prison's guards. The other two clones were [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the course of the fight. Along with [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], whom Ti found inside the prison's solitary confinement, Ti decided to infiltrate Tok's fortress and perform acts of [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] in order to assist the Republic forces in their assault. After Ryyk was killed by a [[Scrange/Legends|scrange]] creature on approach to the fortress, the trooper was assigned to take out the fortress's [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]] single-handedly, while the others would try to take out Tok's powerful [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], or capture the clan leader himself. The trooper succeeded in reaching his target but was fatally wounded by Tok's soldiers who guarded the generator. After Tok's men left him for dead, the trooper rigged his weapon on overload, and it exploded, destroying the generator and killing the trooper in the process. The trooper's sacrifice was one of the key factors that allowed the Republic to win the battle. ==Biography== {{Quote|It's time to split our forces. Trooper, since we have lost the Wookiee, you will have to attack the shield generator on your own.|Shaak Ti|}} A [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], a [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] was bred on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] to serve the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] alongside his clone brethren in the ranks of its [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]]. In [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], the clone [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] participated in a [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Clone Wars)|battle]] on the planet [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]], during which the Republic tried to quell an insurgency led by [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]]-allied clan leader [[Shogar Tok]]. The loss of Brentaal meant the loss of access to the vital [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] for the Republic, and the impeding of passage into the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]]. The battle began after the Republic received intelligence bearing the signature of [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] working undercover as a criminal named "Korto Vos." The message assured the [[Republic High Command/Legends|Republic command]] that the way was clear for an attack and there would be no resistance from the rebels. Upon receiving this information, the Republic immediately followed with an attack on Brentaal IV. [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] oversaw the invasion from a Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], while [[General/Legends|Generals]] [[Agen Kolar/Legends|Agen Kolar]], [[Shon Kon Ray]], and [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]] led the ground forces. The clone trooper fought among Ti's [[Troop/Legends|troops]] during the battle. [[File:Ti_Brentaal.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The trooper fights beside Shaak Ti on Brentaal IV.]] However, the intelligence report had little to do with reality, and the Republic [[Army/Legends|army]] faced heavy resistance from the rebels, who were assisted by Confederate [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. Tok himself commanded his troops from a ground [[Shogar Tok's fortress|fortress]], which harassed Republic landing troops with fire from its powerful [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], forcing Koon to recall all remaining [[Landing craft/Legends|landing craft]] from the surface. The fortress itself was surrounded by an [[Deflector shield/Legends|energy shield]], protecting it from [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]]. Within the first few moments of the battle, the Republic lost more than a quarter of its forces, including General Kon Ray. As they fought the enemy, Ti, the trooper, and two of his comrades were cut off from the rest of Ti's forces. Koon then informed Ti of a [[Brentaal IV prison|prison facility]] located just north of their position. Ti planned to occupy the building and rally her forces from there. The trooper and his two clone brothers followed the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] General to the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] building, witnessing from afar that a battle was raging there. Once Ti and the troopers entered the prison, they found three escaped [[Prisoner/Legends|inmates]]—the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Sagoro Autem]], the [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]] [[Lyshaa]], and the berserk [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Ryyk (Wookiee)|Ryyk]]—fighting the guards. Ti ordered the troopers to attack the guards, and they complied. In the ensuing [[Lightfight|firefight]], one of the other troopers was cut down by [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire and the other one [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the maddened Ryyk. The trooper himself suffered the loss of his [[Phase I clone trooper armor/Legends|helmet]], which was slashed by the Wookiee's [[Sword/Legends|machete]]. Ti then used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Telekinesis/Legends|slam]] Ryyk into a wall, knocking him out, while the trooper and the inmates dealt with the remaining guards. As Ryyk started to come to his senses, the trooper asked permission from Ti to restrain him, but the Jedi instead calmed him down with the help of the Force. [[Force sense/Legends|Sensing]] another presence inside the prison, Ti went to the solitary confinement, where she found Vos, who revealed to her that he had not been the one who sent the transmission. Intent on completing her mission, Ti devised a three-way plan. She, the trooper, Vos, and the inmates would split into three groups, and, using Vos's knowledge of the access points and the layout of Tok's fortress, they would attempt to either capture Tok, [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] the fortress's shields, or take out the ion cannons. If any of those objectives was to succeed, it would greatly improve the Republic's chances of winning the fight. [[File:CloneTrooperSacrifice-ShaakTi.jpg|thumb|right|120px|The trooper sacrifices his life to destroy the shield generator.]] Ti contacted General Kolar, who was still fighting on the surface, telling him to attack the fortress from the north, distracting its defenders, while the sabotage teams would approach from the south. Koon, meanwhile, would seemingly withdraw all his forces, only to return four [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] later when the sabotage had been performed. Autem, Lyshaa, and Ryyk were initially reluctant to participate in the sabotage, but they agreed when Ti promised them amnesty, despite the fact that Lyshaa had been serving her [[Time/Legends|time]] for murdering Ti's [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Fe Sun]]. The group then set off toward Tok's fortress, going through the sewers, where they were attacked by a [[Scrange/Legends|scrange]] creature, which killed Ryyk. With an uneven number of operatives left, the trooper was assigned to attack the fortress's [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] by himself. While Ti and Lyshaa left to confront Tok, Vos and Autem—whose target was the cannons—accompanied the trooper for some of the way before they had to split up. On their way, the three witnessed a number of Brentaal rebels moving captured Republic clone troopers. Seeing this as an opportunity, Vos and Autem came up with a plan to fool the guards. The trooper posed as their prisoner, while the two moved toward the rebels. Autem, who had been a [[Senate Guard/Legends|Senate Guard]] and thus accustomed to how security worked, told the rebels that he and Vos were transporting the "prisoner" for questioning, and the rebels let the three through. After that, Vos gave the trooper final directions to the shield generator, and the team split up. The trooper managed to reach the generator unnoticed but found it heavily guarded; Lyshaa had switched sides and informed Tok of the infiltration teams, and he had alerted the fortress's guards. With time running out, the trooper charged the guards with his [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] blazing, cutting down several of them before being blasted onto the generator core by a shot from a [[Grenade launcher/Legends|grenade launcher]] and losing his consciousness from heavy wounds. The guards believed him to be dead and left the generator room, and soon after that the trooper regained consciousness. Knowing his injuries to be fatal, and determined to finish the mission at all costs, he rigged his blaster to overload and remained seated on the generator as his weapon blew up, annihilating both him and the generator. That, combined with Vos's and Autem's successful destruction of the cannons, Ti's killing of Tok, and Lyshaa's death by one of the fortress's traps, ensured the conquest of Brentaal IV by the Republic forces. ==Personality and traits== {{Dialogue|Vos|Trooper, this is where we split up. Take the left corridor while we go up.|Autem|And try not to talk so much.|The trooper|Roger.|attr=Sagoro Autem, making fun of the trooper's silent nature|src=}} Like all clone troopers, the trooper stood 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. He had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. The trooper was cautious, offering to Ti to restrain Ryyk, who had killed one of the clones and damaged the trooper's [[Armor/Legends|helmet]]. He was generally very quiet and not talkative, a fact that Autem jokingly acknowledged by telling him not to talk so much right before they parted ways on the way to their respective sabotage targets. The trooper was a loyal [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]], and he was willing to sacrifice his life in the name of the Republic. Before his valiant''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' death, the trooper took solace in the fact that he had done his duty to the best of his ability. ==Behind the scenes== This clone trooper first appeared, unnamed, in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] ''[[Jedi: Shaak Ti]]'', written by [[John Ostrander]] and illustrated by [[Jan Duursema]], and published on [[May 14]], [[2003]]. In the original comic book, the Battle of Brentaal was said to have taken place five [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]], placing it in the year [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. However, the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]] provided the date of the battle, and therefore the clone trooper's death, as 21 BBY. This article uses the date provided by the ''Essential Atlas'', due to it being a more recent source referencing the event. ==Appearances== *''[[Jedi: Shaak Ti]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|67|Comics Link}} {{Po}} *{{FactFile|116|SHA 7-8|Shaak Ti}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Soldado clon no identificado (Brentaal IV) |pl=Klon-szeregowy (Brentaal IV) }} [[Category:Clone troopers]]
